subject: Mathematics
title: What Is Logic?
description: A quick, practical guide to What Is Logic?.

hook.text: Every argument you've ever made follows invisible rules. Logic is the study of those rules.

buildup.text: Logic asks: when does a conclusion truly follow from its premises? Not all reasoning is valid.

discovery.text: Aristotle formalized logic in 350 BC with syllogisms: 'All men are mortal; Socrates is a man; therefore Socrates is mortal.'

twist.text: An argument can be logically valid even if its premises are false. Validity is about structure, not truth.

climax.text: Logic underpins mathematics, computer science, law, and every field that requires rigorous reasoning.

punchline.text: Logic is the grammar of clear thinking.

quiz.question: Can a logically valid argument have false premises?
options: Yes, validity is about structure not truth
